title: Core Game Loop & Update Tick
entry_point: ApplicationListener.update() called each frame by the arc engine for all registered listeners: Logic, Control, Renderer (in that order via Vars registration)

steps: [if !client && !editor] for each active team: fillItems, buildAi.update(), rtsAi.update(), prebuildAi spawn
[if !client && wavetime <= 0 && waves] runWave() → spawner.spawnEnemies(), state.wave++, Events.fire(WaveEvent)
Compute envAttrs from rules + active weather groups
Groups.update() — update all entity groups (units, buildings, bullets, weather...)
NaN guard: if player.x/y NaN → reset position; if camera NaN → reset to map center
[if !client && core != null && campaign] unlock core items
[if paused && isCutscene] state.set(playing) — cutscene forces unpause
[if pause key tapped && conditions met] toggle paused/playing
[if menu key tapped] show pause menu / chat hide
Read camera scale target; lerp camerascale toward dest (0.1f factor)
Read settings: laser opacity, shield/water animation flags, bloom, pixelate, etc.
[if launchAnimator != null && landTime <= 0] launchAnimator.endLaunch(); launchAnimator = null
camera.width = graphics.width / camerascale; camera.height = graphics.height / camerascale
Register layered draw calls (sorted by Layer z-value):
[if bloom] capture at bullet layer, render at effect layer

branching_conditions: state.isGame() — main gate for both Logic and Control game-mode branches
state.isPaused() — skips entity updates in Logic; skips indicators/pause-toggle in Control
net.client() — skips enemy counting, weather, wave spawning, game-over checks on clients
state.isCampaign() — enables sector info update, universe.update(), item unlocking
state.rules.fog — gates fogControl.update() in Logic and fog.drawFog() in Renderer
renderer.pixelate — switches between pixelator.drawPixelate() and full draw() in Renderer
landTime > 0 — cutscene mode: overrides camera scale, disables weather, blocks pause
state.isMenu() — Renderer skips game draw; Control runs Time.update() and back-key only
key_relationships: Logic implements ApplicationListener — update() is engine-driven per frame
Control implements ApplicationListener — update() is engine-driven per frame
Renderer implements ApplicationListener — update() is engine-driven per frame
Logic.update calls Groups.update() — updates all game entity groups
Logic.update calls Time.update() inside game tick
Control.update delegates to input.update(), input.updateState(), input.updateSelectQuadtree()
Control.update delegates to saves.update(), sound.update(), indicators.update()
Renderer.update calls minimap.update() and draw() each non-menu frame
Renderer.draw() calls blocks.checkChanges/processBlocks/drawBlocks, overlays, lights, fog, bloom
Renderer depends_on Control (reads control.input for cutscene/drawCommanded)
Logic fires Events (Trigger.update, beforeGameUpdate, afterGameUpdate) consumed by other systems
GameState is shared mutable state read by all three: state.isGame, isPaused, tick, wave, enemies
important_notes: All three classes implement arc.ApplicationListener; the engine calls update() on each per render frame. Logic.update() runs first and advances simulation; Control.update() processes input and UI state; Renderer.update() then draws. The isPaused check in Logic skips all simulation (Groups.update, Time.update, wave timer) but still calls checkGameState. The cutscene (landTime > 0) in Renderer overrides camerascale and blocks normal weather but does not pause Logic. runWave() is called directly from Logic.update() when wavetime reaches zero (server-side only).
